source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
John has made a building of unit cubes standing on a $4 \times 4$ grid. The diagram shows the number of cubes standing on each cell. When John looks horizontally at the building from behind, what does he see? <image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field only contains option labels without option text
question text includes image placeholders <image1> and <image2>

issues: Choice option text is missing; metadata notes that options only contain labels without option text.
Question text contains two image placeholders (<image1> and <image2>) but only one image path is provided.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'The diagram shows the number of cubes standing on each cell' and asks what is seen 'from behind', so the visual layout is essential.
The task depends on spatial/visual properties of stacked unit cubes on a 4x4 grid.
Structured choices only contain labels (A-E), so the meaningful option content is likely image-based rather than text-based.
multi_step: Solving requires reading the cube heights from the diagram and then transforming that 3D arrangement into a horizontal rear view.
The rear view requires combining information across multiple grid cells and comparing stack heights to determine the visible profile.
This is a visual-spatial reasoning task with at least two linked steps: interpret the grid, then project/match the resulting view to an option.
verifiability: The final answer format is a single option label ('C'), so the target output is unambiguous.
Given a complete diagram and answer options, the rear-view silhouette can be checked by rule-based visual/spatial reasoning.
